Speech-Language Pathologist License in Idaho

Here's what you need to know.

Last updated 2026-02-25 · Sources verified

At a glance

Endorsement fee
$75
Processing time
2 to 4 weeks
Renewal fee
$75
Fingerprinting
Required
Board
Bureau of Occupational Licenses — Speech and Hearing Services
Path
ASLP-IC active
See the steps Primary board page

Last updated 2026-02-25.

Out-of-State Speech-Language Pathologists

Idaho is an active member of the ASLP-IC. If your home state also participates and your license is eligible, you can obtain a compact privilege to practice in Idaho.

If you do not qualify for the compact, you can apply for licensure using the traditional endorsement pathway detailed below.

Key Timeline Factors

Keep these requirements in mind, as they are the most likely to impact your processing timeline in Idaho.

  • Fingerprinting: Required. Out-of-state applicants can use IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects). Completing prints outside Idaho can add 2-4 weeks.
  • License verification: The board does not spell out a separate license verification step for this path.
  • Compact status: ASLP-IC is active in Idaho.
  • Temporary license: The board lists a temporary or provisional route while the full file is pending.

Idaho Endorsement Requirements

This is the step-by-step endorsement checklist for already-licensed speech-language pathologists applying to practice in Idaho.

  1. Step 1

    Submit Your Application

    Apply through the Bureau of Occupational Licenses — Speech and Hearing Services. The application fee is $75.

  2. Step 2

    Request Official graduate transcripts

    From a CAA-accredited program.

  3. Step 3

    Submit Your Praxis exam score report

    Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.

  4. Step 4

    Verify Your Clinical Fellowship verification

    CF completion documentation.

  5. Step 5

    Provide Background check

    Criminal background check.

  6. Step 6

    Complete Fingerprinting and Background Screening

    Idaho requires a criminal background check for all applicants. Out-of-state applicants can use IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects). Completing prints outside Idaho can add 2-4 weeks to your processing time.

  7. Step 7

    Await Board Processing

    Plan for a processing timeline of about 2 to 4 weeks once your file is complete. If you need to start working immediately, the board offers a temporary or provisional licensing route that you can utilize while your full application is pending.

Fees, Processing Time, and Renewal

Here is a complete breakdown of the costs and timelines associated with getting and maintaining your Idaho Speech-Language Pathologist license.

Initial Licensing Costs

Application fee
$75
Background check
$0 (included in application) (The board's processing fee is included in the application, but out-of-state fingerprinting services charge a separate fee of ~$40)
Total estimated cost
$75 (Covers your application fee plus the initial license issuance fee)

Processing Timeline

Estimated time
2 to 4 weeks (once your application and fingerprinting are fully received by the board)

Ongoing License Renewal

Renewal cycle
Every year
Renewal fee
$75

Temporary License

Idaho offers a temporary or provisional license so you can start working while your full endorsement application is processing.

Board Contact

Department
Idaho Bureau of Occupational Licenses
Phone
(208) 334-3233
Email
ibol@ibol.idaho.gov
Board site
Primary board page
Compact source
ASLP-IC

Source: Primary board website

Idaho Speech-Language Pathologist Licensing FAQ

How much does an Idaho SLP license cost?

The application and license fee is $75. Idaho has among the lowest SLP licensing fees. Annual renewal is $75.

Is Idaho part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?

Yes. Idaho has enacted ASLP-IC legislation.

Does Idaho allow telepractice for SLPs?

Yes. Idaho permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and compact privilege holders.

Does Idaho have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

Yes. Idaho participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Idaho without a separate endorsement application.

How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Idaho?

Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

Can I start working in Idaho while my endorsement application is processing?

A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

This page is maintained as a reference for speech-language pathologists and recruiters evaluating Idaho assignments.
